$comment: Tool declarations for the go ratchet. go_build is the oracle: it type-checks Go source from stdin with `go build`. The engine dispatches the .sh script to bash by extension; on Windows add a go_check.ps1 and a second tool entry (or swap the script field).

tools.name: go_build
tools.description: Type-check a complete Go source file with `go build` (as a library package) and report OK or the build diagnostics. The Go source is supplied on stdin.

tools.name: go_test
tools.description: BEHAVIOR oracle: type-check, `go vet`, and `go test` a Go package. The payload (on stdin) carries two files introduced by markers: `// === solution.go ===` (the implementation, `package solution`) and `// === solution_test.go ===` (the tests). Exit 0 iff vet is clean and the tests pass. Requires a *_test.go file (a package with no tests is a silent false pass).

code.description: two marker-separated Go files: solution.go and solution_test.go

tools.name: doctor_go
tools.description: Toolbelt probe for `ratchet doctor` (analog of cpp's doctor_cl): reports the Go toolbelt and its optional linters. Exit 0 if the required `go` toolchain (build/vet/test) is present, 1 if not. Optional linters are reported as info, never block. Run directly with `/do doctor_go`.

tools.name: new_module
tools.description: Scaffold a Go module workspace for composition under workspaces/<name> (go.mod, PROJECT.md, specs/). Every composed unit is a file in `package main` at the module root. Arg: spec = the module name. Then drop .spec files in workspaces/<name>/specs and run `compose --ws <name>`.

tools.name: read_specs
tools.description: Read every .spec file in a directory (or one .spec file) and print contents labeled by filename, for compose's plan step and add_unit's per-unit read. Arg: dir-or-file (workspace-relative paths resolve under workspaces/).

tools.name: module_api
tools.description: Emit the existing API surface of a composed module: every top-level type/func and method declaration in its .go files, labeled by file - the authoritative names a new unit must call verbatim. Arg: proj (workspace name).

tools.name: read_module
tools.description: Show a composed module's state for grounding: PROJECT.md, go.mod, and the .go file tree. Arg: proj (workspace name).

tools.name: stage_build
tools.description: Compose per-unit oracle: write a generated Go file into a module workspace and build the WHOLE module (go build ./...). Exit 0 iff the whole module compiles. Args: proj, path; the Go source on stdin.

path.description: target file under the module, e.g. main.go or warehouse.go

tools.name: plan_units
tools.description: Turn a compose build-plan JSON (on stdin) into the foreach worklist: one line per unit in dependency order, '<targetpath> <specfile>'. Entry (role behavior/gui) -> main.go; others -> <name>.go at the module root.

tools.name: register_file
tools.description: Record a composed unit in the module's PROJECT.md changelog (idempotent, best-effort, always exits 0). Args: proj, path, role.

tools.name: module_check
tools.description: Whole-module FINAL oracle for compose: go build ./... then go test ./... over the entire module. Exit 0 iff both pass (behavior, not just compilation). Arg: proj.

tools.name: stage_check
tools.description: Project-lifecycle oracle (add_file/edit_file): write a generated Go file into a workspace, then go vet ./... + go test ./... over the WHOLE module. Exit 0 iff both pass. Uses vet (not build) so it works whether or not the module has a func main yet. Args: proj, path; the Go source on stdin.

path.description: target file under the workspace, e.g. parser.go

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Print one workspace file verbatim, to ground a surgical edit on its exact current contents. Args: proj, path. Exits 1 if the file is missing.

tools.name: log_edit
tools.description: Append an 'edited' changelog line to a workspace's PROJECT.md (best-effort, always exits 0). Args: proj, path, summary.

tools.name: add_dep
tools.description: Add a third-party Go module to a workspace AND ingest its API into the `deps` KB so the lifecycle/compose flows ground on its real surface. Runs go get + go mod tidy in the workspace, writes go doc -all to kb/deps/<slug>.md, re-indexes. Needs network. Args: proj (workspace), mod (import path, optionally @version). Run via /do add_dep <proj> <mod>.

tools.name: run_app
tools.description: Build and RUN a workspace's main, capturing stdout/stderr/exit (the go run / run_app analog). A program that exits reports its real exit code; a blocking server is stopped after the timeout and reported as still-running. Observes behavior; the build/tests remain the oracle. Args: proj, optional secs (default 3). Run via /do run_app <proj>.

tools.name: spec_check
tools.description: Spec oracle: validate that marker-separated .spec drafts (on stdin) are well-formed - each needs a name:, a valid role: (data/interface/component/behavior/gui/test), and at least one of api:/behavior:/intent:. Exit 0 iff all valid. No side effects. Run via /do spec_check.

tools.name: write_specs
tools.description: Validate (via spec_check) marker-separated .spec drafts (on stdin) and, if all well-formed, write each into <proj>/specs/ (bootstrapping go.mod + specs/ if absent). Atomic: writes nothing if any spec is invalid. Args: proj; specs on stdin.

tools.name: go_quality
tools.description: Production gate: run the full quality/security suite over a workspace (gofmt, go vet, go build, go test -race, staticcheck, govulncheck, gosec) and exit 0 only if every present tool is clean. Absent tools are skipped. govulncheck needs network. Arg: proj. Run via /do go_quality <proj> or the harden flow.

tools.name: mine_runs
tools.description: Self-improvement engine: scan runs/ for recurring oracle failures, cluster them into error classes, and flag which are NOT yet covered by kb/pitfalls (KB candidates). Run via /do mine_runs.

tools.name: write_pitfall
tools.description: Oracle+writer for the learn flow: validate a drafted kb/pitfalls entry (markdown on stdin: needs an H1 title + a ```go block) and write it to kb/pitfalls, reindexing. Exit 1 if malformed.

tools.name: link_repo
tools.description: Work on an existing Go module outside workspaces/: symlink it in as workspaces/<name> so the lifecycle flows operate on the real repo. Args: name, path (module dir with go.mod). Run via /do link_repo <name> <path>.

tools.name: gorename
tools.description: Type-safe rename across a workspace module (gorename): renames every reference by type across files, then verifies the build. Args: proj, target (name or Type.Method), newname. Run via /do gorename <proj> <target> <new>.

tools.name: bump
tools.description: Verified version change for a workspace (with rollback): snapshot go.mod/go.sum, apply the change, go mod tidy, then run go_quality; keep only if clean, else roll back. Args: proj, change (<pkg>@<ver>, go=<ver>, -u, or tidy). Run via /do bump <proj> <change>.

tools.name: read_files
tools.description: Print several workspace files, each under a === path === marker, as input to a coordinated multi-file edit. Args: proj, comma-separated file list.

tools.name: stage_files
tools.description: Transactional multi-file oracle: write marker-separated Go files (stdin), verify the whole module (go vet + go test -race), keep only if clean else roll back every touched file. Arg: proj; payload on stdin.

tools.name: kb_catalog
tools.description: Generate kb/catalog.json, the high-level KB routing catalog (one entry per knowledge library: name, path, doc-count, default, one-line subject summary) that the search-time route walk reads. Structure is regenerated from ratchet.json + each manifest (cannot drift); authored summaries are preserved. Modes via {mode}: empty=write, --list=print 'name: summary' lines for a plan prompt, --check=exit 1 if stale or any summary empty.

tools.name: route_score
tools.description: Deterministic oracle for model-driven KB routing: given the task as {query} and the planner's KB selections (JSON on stdin, or KB names as extra args), score each pick's grounding against its catalog summary + manifest keywords and check coverage (a discriminating query term held by an unpicked KB = a dropped-relevant-branch). Exit 0 if picks are plausible and coverage is complete; exit 1 to make the engine widen/backtrack (re-plan).

tools.name: pack_refs
tools.description: Assemble the route flow's retrieved grounding (sentinel-marked <<<kb>>> sections on stdin) into a clean context pack, dropping empty sections. The output of route: a verified, grounded context.

tools.name: tdd_red
tools.description: RED oracle for the tdd flow: write the test file(s) (=== path.go === markers on stdin) into <proj> and prove the test compiles against the stub (go vet) AND fails (go test non-zero, stub panics). Exit 0 iff red achieved; rejects a test that passes against the stub (tests nothing).

tools.name: go_fuzz
tools.description: Property/fuzz rung: run every native fuzz target (FuzzXxx) in <proj> for a bounded time (env FUZZTIME, default 5s) to catch edge-case bugs example tests miss. Exit 1 on any crash; 0 if clean or no targets.

tools.name: tdd_cap
tools.description: Bound a tdd repair cycle: `tdd_cap <proj> <name> <max>` increments a per-cycle counter (.tdd/<name>) and exits 0 while count<=max (keep looping) or 1 once exceeded (give up -> route to fail). `tdd_cap <proj> reset` clears counters at run start.
